seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Data Extraction pipelines with Semantic Kernel enables schema-guided LLM calls to parse unstructured documents into structured JSON outputs. Guardrails are essential to prevent cascading errors from plugin chaining, invalid plans, hallucinated memories, and data contamination that can undermine extraction reliability. Vex integration as a kernel filter ensures schema compliance, field accuracy, completeness, and type consistency in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture begins with document ingestion, followed by Semantic Kernel's planner selecting extraction plugins based on schema definitions. Plugins invoke LLMs with schema-guided prompts to extract fields like names, dates, and amounts from unstructured text. Results flow through memory for context retention and chaining for multi-step extractions.

Planners generate execution graphs that chain native functions, custom plugins, and OpenAI functions. Memory stores prior extractions for iterative refinement, while filters validate outputs against Pydantic schemas. Failures occur when plans omit required fields or memory contaminates cross-document data.

Vex integrates as a post-function result filter, verifying extracted JSON against predefined tests before returning to the kernel.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Sequential plugin calls where early extraction failures propagate invalid data to downstream plugins
risks.mitigation: Vex result filters validate each plugin output before chaining

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Planner selects incompatible plugins or omits required schema fields
risks.mitigation: Pre-validate plan steps with Vex schema compliance checks

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Kernel memory injects fabricated prior extractions into current document processing
risks.mitigation: Vex field accuracy tests against ground truth schemas

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: Data from one extraction plugin leaks into unrelated schema fields
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness and type consistency verification

implementation.title: Define Extraction Schema with Pydantic
implementation.description: Create schema for invoice extraction that Semantic Kernel plugins will target

implementation.title: Create Semantic Kernel Extraction Plugin
implementation.description: Native function plugin that uses schema-guided LLM call
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import ContextVariables, Kernel
from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion

async def extract_invoice(
    kernel: Kernel,
    context: ContextVariables
) -> str:
    document_text = context["document"]
    schema = Invoice.model_json_schema()
    
    prompt = f"""
Extract invoice data from this document as valid JSON matching this schema:
{schema}

Document: {document_text}

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:"""
    
    chat_service = OpenAIChatCompletion(
        deployment_name="gpt-4",
        ai_model_id="gpt-4"
    )
    result = await kernel.invoke_prompt_async(
        prompt, chat_service, context
    )
    return str(result)

vexIntegration.title: Python Planner with Vex Guardrails
vexIntegration.description: Sequential planner with Vex validation at each step
vexIntegration.code:
from semantic_kernel.planning import SequentialPlanner

from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

planner = SequentialPlanner(kernel)

# Plan with Vex validation steps
plan = await planner.create_plan_async(goal="Extract invoice from document")

for step in plan._steps:
    result = await kernel.invoke_async(step, document=doc_text)
    
    # Vex validates each step result
    extracted = json.loads(str(result))
    vex.verify("invoice_extraction", extracted)
    
    # Update context for next step
    step.output_variable = extracted
